title: Circular measure
summary: 9709 P1 radians — arc length, sector area, and angular measure with GeoGebra unit circle.
learningObjectives: Calculate arc length and sector area.
Use radian measure in trigonometry and calculus.

simpleExplanation.title: Radians instead of degrees
simpleExplanation.summary: One radian is the angle when arc length equals radius. Calculus and P3 trig require radians.
steps: 180° = π rad; multiply degrees by π/180.
Arc s = rθ; sector area = ½r²θ.
ω = θ/t; linear speed v = rω.

sections.content: **Circular measure** (1.4) is assumed in all later trig. Examiners penalise **degree mode** errors in calculus questions.
